content.problemStatement:
WW3 is near and Gru wants to recruit minions for his team. Gru went to the planet of minions to recruit minions, he saw that there are two villages separated by a river. He cannot recruit minions from both villages because then his team will have internal conflicts.

Gru is now in a dilemma about which village to recruit as he wants to have the strongest possible team.

You are given coordinates of houses on the planet. Each house has exactly one minion and his power is given. The planet of minion is considered as a 2-D plane and the river is denoted by a straight line ( y=mx+cy=mx+cy=mx+c ).
Note:Note:Note: None of the houses are situated on the river.
###Input:


First-line will contain NNN, number of houses.
Second-line will contain two integers, mmm and ccc denoting the river.
Next NNN lines will have exactly 3 integers X[i],Y[i],P[i]X[i], Y[i], P[i]X[i],Y[i],P[i] denoting the coordinates of houses and the power of minion in that house
###Output:


Print the maximum power of the team which Gru can recruit.
###Constraints


1‚Č§N‚Č§1051 \leq N \leq 10^51‚Č§N‚Č§105
‚ąí104‚Č§m,c‚Č§104-10^4 \leq m,c \leq 10^4‚ąí104‚Č§m,c‚Č§104
‚ąí104‚Č§X[i],Y[i]‚Č§104-10^4 \leq X[i], Y[i] \leq 10^4‚ąí104‚Č§X[i],Y[i]‚Č§104
1‚Č§P[i]‚Č§104 1 \leq P[i] \leq 10^41‚Č§P[i]‚Č§104
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer N, number of houses. Each of the next N lines contains three integers X, Y, P denoting the coordinates of houses and the power of minion in that house. The last line contains two integers m and c denoting the river.
content.outputFormat: Print the maximum power of the team which Gru can recruit.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a simple sweep line algorithm. We first sort the houses by their x-coordinates. Then, we iterate over the houses and for each house, we check if it is on the left side of the river or the right side. If it is on the left side, we add its power to the maximum power. If it is on the right side, we subtract its power from the maximum power. We also keep track of the maximum power seen so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) due to sorting

code.Python3:
import sys
import math

N = int(input())
m, c = map(int, input().split())
houses = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(N)]
maxPower = 0
for x, y, p in houses:
    if y - m * x > c:
        maxPower = max(maxPower, p)
    else:
        maxPower = max(maxPower, -p)
print(maxPower)
